claim: Austral demonstrates a deliberately simple ownership discipline: a linear type system defined by two rules (linear universe + use-once), explicitly rejecting Rust-style evolving ownership analysis, with scope-delineated borrows and no lifetime annotations, under a spec philosophy that a programmer can learn the whole language from the spec.
positive_scope: Existence proof that a one-implementor-scale checker for ownership-class safety is designable by sacrificing ergonomics/terseness — exactly the axis D0 permits sacrificing.
negative_scope: Austral is more restrictive than Rust (some sound patterns need restructuring); spec-level claims, no card yet on implementation experience at scale; linearity alone is weaker than full borrow expressiveness.

claim: Polonius formulates Rust borrow checking as datalog rules over extracted facts (alias-based formulation), indicating the core analysis is expressible declaratively and compactly, separate from rustc engineering.
positive_scope: Directional evidence that borrow-checking logic can be small when expressed as rules over explicit facts.
negative_scope: Intro page only; rule count/fact schemas not yet carded — needs the Rules chapter and the 2018 alias-based-formulation post; Polonius targets full Rust, more than our core needs.

claim: Featherweight Rust (Pearce, TOPLAS 2021) is a lightweight calculus covering copy/move semantics, mutable borrowing, reborrowing, partial moves, and lifetimes, with a formal soundness proof, a Java reference implementation, model checking against 500+ billion generated programs, and fuzzing against rustc that found one confirmed rustc bug.
positive_scope: Primary candidate for the section-5 formal-calculus reconciliation: soundness-proven, deliberately lightweight, single-implementor reference implementation — directly matches the D1a levers, and its model-check/fuzz methodology is a template for our checker validation.
negative_scope: A simplified calculus, not a complete formalization of rustc; our region model differs (explicit named regions vs FR lifetimes) so reconciliation is a mapping exercise, not adoption verbatim.

claim: Oxide (arXiv 1903.00982) formalizes source-level Rust with FULLY-ANNOTATED types including non-lexical lifetimes, computing lifetimes via substructural typing, with the first syntactic progress-and-preservation type-safety proof for borrow checking.
positive_scope: The NLL-upgrade reference if OWN-6 lexical liveness is ever relaxed; notably its fully-annotated-types premise matches our no-inference direction.
negative_scope: Closer to full Rust than our kernel needs; abstract gives no reference-implementation evidence.
